channel.title: NRIBeat — Daily Pulse for Indian Americans
channel.description: Immigration, AI, Cricket, Bollywood, and Layoffs — curated daily for the Indian-American community.

item.title: KiloClaw: Enterprise AI Security for Indian Tech Teams in 2026
item.description: Kilo's KiloClaw addresses the "shadow AI" problem where developers use unauthorized AI tools on personal infrastructure. For Indian engineers in US tech roles, this is critical: it creates a legal way to use powerful AI without risking compliance violations or job security. Early adoption signals seniority and could directly impact your next role and salary negotiations.

item.title: Anthropic's GitHub Takedown: What It Means for AI Engineers in 2026
item.description: Anthropic accidentally mass-removed thousands of GitHub repos while targeting its leaked source code—a mistake now retracted. For you: API users are safe, but this accelerates the shift toward open-source AI tools like Llama. If you're building your ML career in 2026, multi-tool expertise (not single-platform dependency) is now the competitive advantage.

item.title: EB-2 India May 2026 Visa Bulletin Prediction: Modest Forward Movement Expected
item.description: We predict EB-2 India will advance 10-16 days in May 2026, landing around July 25-31, 2014, with 72% confidence. This represents typical May-period steady movement with low retrogression risk, as the fiscal year is still in mid-phase.

item.title: Bollywood vs Hollywood: IMAX Screen Wars Heat Up in 2025
item.description: Project Hail Mary, Dhurandhar: The Revenge Round 2, and The Super Mario Galaxy Movie are battling for IMAX screens in India. For NRIs in the US, these films will arrive on streaming platforms (Disney+ Hotstar, Netflix, Prime Video) within 30-90 days of theatrical release. Expect Bollywood entries on Hotstar first.

item.title: Rassie van der Dussen Retires: Impact on India Cricket & IPL 2025
item.description: South African batter Rassie van der Dussen has retired from international cricket but will continue playing franchise cricket (potentially IPL 2025) and mentoring young cricketers. This marks a shift in how elite players structure their careers post-international cricket. US-based cricket fans should watch for his potential IPL involvement, which offers more convenient viewing times than international tours.

item.title: Australia Zimbabwe ODI Series 2026: India Cricket Context
item.description: Australia tours Zimbabwe for 3 ODIs in September 2026—their first visit in 8 years. While not directly India cricket, this series impacts global rankings and Australian squad planning ahead of future India vs Australia matches. Indian diaspora fans should track it for context on upcoming tournaments.

item.title: England Women's New Spin Coach: What It Means for India Cricket 2026
item.description: England Women hired former Gloucestershire spinner Tom Smith as their new spin-bowling coach to work with Sophie Ecclestone and other elite spinners ahead of the T20 World Cup. This signals how seriously top teams are investing in specialized coaching—a trend that will impact India cricket and women's international competition in 2026.

item.title: April 2026 Visa Bulletin: EB-2 India Final Action Date Holds Steady to July 15, 2014
item.description: April 2026 Visa Bulletin: EB-2 India Final Action Date holds steady to July 15, 2014; EB-3 India to November 15, 2013. Dates for Filing: EB-2 January 15, 2015, EB-3 January 15, 2015. No movement this month — monitor the next bulletin closely.

item.title: Call Me Bae Season 2: Shruti Haasan Joins Ananya Panday in Thailand
item.description: Shruti Haasan is joining Ananya Panday's hit Netflix web series Call Me Bae for Season 2, currently filming in Thailand. The show will premiere on Netflix in 2025. Here's where to watch and what to expect from this exciting expansion of the beloved Indian web series.

item.title: Amelia Kerr's 179* Leads NZ Cricket Record Chase vs South Africa
item.description: Amelia Kerr's unbeaten 179 led New Zealand to the highest successful run chase in women's ODI history—347 runs against South Africa. Indian cricket fans in the US can catch this and all India cricket matches on Willow TV, Disney+ Hotstar, or ESPN+.

item.title: India vs Australia Test 2025: CA Contract Updates Before Tour
item.description: Cricket Australia has announced its contract list for the India tour, bringing in batter Jason Weatherald and fast bowler Michael Neser while dropping Joel Richardson. This signals Australia's shift toward conditions-specific selections for Indian pitches ahead of the five-Test series in early 2025.

item.title: Rajesh Khanna Legal Case: What Bollywood Fans Should Know
item.description: The Bombay High Court dismissed Anita Advani's legal claim seeking marital recognition with late superstar Rajesh Khanna, closing a decade-long legal battle. The ruling has no impact on Khanna's cinematic legacy, which remains accessible through Netflix, Prime Video, and Hotstar for diaspora audiences. This case highlights Indian legal processes around marriage recognition while reminding fans that cinema—not courtrooms—is where legends truly live on.

item.title: India Cricket Tour Zimbabwe 2026: T20I Schedule & US Watch Guide
item.description: India will tour Zimbabwe for three T20Is in July 2026, with Zimbabwe returning to India for three ODIs in January 2027 (their first bilateral series in India since 2002). US-based NRI cricket fans can catch the action on Willow TV with early morning ET broadcasts. This bilateral commitment follows the excitement of IPL 2025 and provides crucial player rotation opportunities for India.

item.title: Pretext: The Open Source Web Design Standard That Changes Dev Jobs [2026]
item.description: Pretext, a new open-source web standard, eliminates the 30-year layout reflow bottleneck that slows down modern web apps. For Indian engineers at US tech companies, adopting Pretext now is a career accelerator—it improves code performance, integrates seamlessly with AI tools for developers, and will become a required skill within 2-3 years.

item.title: India vs Australia Test 2026: Green Backed for Long-Term Role
item.description: Cameron Green gets continued backing from Australia despite his lean Ashes performance, signaling he'll be part of the India vs Australia Test series in August 2026. For NRIBeat readers, this means another redemption arc storyline to follow while catching India cricket matches from the US—here's how to watch live without missing sleep.

item.title: Shreyas Iyer & Gill Return: PBKS vs GT T20 2026
item.description: Shreyas Iyer and Shubman Gill are back in focus as Punjab Kings and Gujarat Titans—two IPL 2025 playoff teams—clash in a critical T20 battle. Both captains need big performances to restore their T20 form. US cricket fans can watch live on Willow TV, ESPN+, or stream highlights next day on YouTube.

item.title: Salman Khan & Nayanthara Team Up for First Time in 2026
item.description: Bollywood megastars Salman Khan and Nayanthara are collaborating for the first time in director Vamshi Paidipally's upcoming action drama. The project, produced by Dil Raju, will likely stream on Netflix, Prime Video, or Disney+ Hotstar after its theatrical run—major news for Indian diaspora seeking new Hindi cinema in 2026.

item.title: Sooryavanshi, Archer Demolish CSK: IPL 2025 Highlights
item.description: CSK crumbled to 127 all out in a devastating IPL 2025 loss as Sooryavanshi, Archer, and Burger dismantled the new-look lineup. This comprehensive bowling display is a major turning point in the tournament, and Indian fans in the US can catch full replays on Willow TV and ESPN+.

item.title: March 2026 Visa Bulletin: EB-2 India Final Action Date Holds Steady to September 15, 2013
item.description: March 2026 Visa Bulletin: EB-2 India Final Action Date holds steady to September 15, 2013; EB-3 India to November 15, 2013. Dates for Filing: EB-2 November 01, 2014, EB-3 August 15, 2014. No movement this month — monitor the next bulletin closely.

item.title: Dhurandhar The Revenge Gets Fixed: Mirror Goof Corrected in New 2025 Prints
item.description: Dhurandhar The Revenge, Ranveer Singh's revenge thriller directed by Aditya Dhar, has been corrected with revised prints after a minor mirror continuity goof was discovered. The film is still in select US theaters and will likely stream on Prime Video or Disney+ Hotstar within 6-8 weeks. Our verdict: worth watching for fans of intelligent action thrillers.
